seoTitle: Recom Products | DC-DC Converters, AC-DC Power Supplies, LED Drivers
seoDescription: Browse Recom's comprehensive power product portfolio including DC-DC converters, AC-DC power supplies, switching regulators, and LED drivers for industrial, medical, and automotive applications.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Recom DC-DC converter for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Recom DC-DC converter involves: 1) Input voltage range - determine your system voltage and choose 2:1 or 4:1 input range options; 2) Output requirements - calculate voltage and current needs with 20-30% margin; 3) Isolation needs - select isolated or non-isolated based on safety requirements; 4) Package type - SIP for through-hole, DIP for dual in-line, SMD for surface mount; 5) Temperature range - ensure rating matches your environment (-40°C to +85°C standard); 6) Certifications - verify medical, automotive, or industrial certifications needed. Recom provides online selection tools and our FAE team can assist with specific applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our product selector or contact FAE for application-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the difference between isolated and non-isolated DC-DC converters?
faqs.answer: Key differences between isolated and non-isolated converters: Isolated converters provide galvanic isolation between input and output using transformers, typically 1000VDC to 6000VDC isolation voltage. They are required for: Safety-critical applications, Breaking ground loops, Level shifting between different voltage domains, Medical equipment requiring patient isolation. Non-isolated converters (like buck, boost, buck-boost) have direct DC connection between input and output. Advantages include: Higher efficiency (no transformer losses), Lower cost, Smaller size, Simpler design. Recom offers both types - isolated converters for safety-critical applications and non-isolated switching regulators for cost-sensitive designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for guidance on isolated vs non-isolated selection for your application.

faqs.question: What packages are available for Recom DC-DC converters?
faqs.answer: Recom DC-DC converters are available in multiple package types: SIP (Single In-line Package) - 7-pin or 8-pin through-hole mounting, compact vertical design; DIP (Dual In-line Package) - 14-pin or 16-pin through-hole, wider footprint for higher power; SMD (Surface Mount Device) - Compact surface mount packages for automated assembly; DIL (Dual In-line) - Similar to DIP with specific pin configurations. Package selection depends on: Board space constraints - SIP for vertical space, SMD for compact designs; Assembly process - through-hole for prototyping, SMD for production; Power dissipation - larger packages for better thermal performance; Application requirements - industrial vs commercial grade. Recom provides detailed mechanical drawings for all packages.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for package recommendations based on your assembly process and space requirements.

faqs.question: What is the difference between 2:1 and 4:1 input voltage ranges?
faqs.answer: Input voltage range options for Recom DC-DC converters: 2:1 input range - covers a 2:1 ratio of input voltage, e.g., 9-18VDC for 12V nominal, 18-36VDC for 24V nominal. Advantages: Slightly higher efficiency, Lower cost, Good for stable input sources. 4:1 input range - covers a 4:1 ratio, e.g., 9-36VDC, 18-75VDC. Advantages: Handles wide input variations, Suitable for battery applications (12V/24V), Accommodates voltage sags and transients, One part number covers multiple input scenarios. Selection depends on your input source stability. For battery-powered or automotive applications, 4:1 is recommended. For stable DC bus applications, 2:1 offers optimal efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team to determine the optimal input range for your power source.

faqs.question: How do I get pricing and availability for Recom products?
faqs.answer: As an authorized Recom distributor, BeiLuo Electronics provides competitive pricing and stock availability. To get pricing: Contact our sales team with your part numbers and quantities; Request volume pricing for production quantities; Ask about scheduled delivery programs for long-term supply. For availability: Check current stock for standard products; Lead times for non-stock items typically 2-4 weeks; Expedite options available for urgent requirements. Technical support and samples are also available through our FAE team. We maintain inventory of popular Recom products for immediate delivery.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team for current pricing, availability, and to discuss your procurement requirements.

categories.name: DC-DC Converters
categories.description: High-efficiency isolated DC-DC converters from 1W to 60W
categories.longDescription: Recom's DC-DC converter portfolio offers industry-leading power density and reliability for demanding applications available through authorized distributors. These isolated converters feature 2:1 or 4:1 input voltage ranges, regulated outputs, and high isolation voltages up to 6000VDC. Available in compact SIP, DIP, and SMD packages, Recom converters are ideal for industrial automation, medical equipment, telecommunications, and transportation applications. Our distributor selection guide helps you choose the right converter series for your application requirements. All converters feature wide operating temperature ranges (-40°C to +85°C without derating), high efficiency (up to 89%), and comprehensive protection features including short circuit, overvoltage, and overtemperature protection. Medical-grade versions offer 2xMOPP isolation meeting IEC 60601-1 standards for patient safety applications.

series.name: RxxCTxx Series
series.description: Compact 2:1 input DC-DC converters in SIP package

series.name: RxxPxxxx Series
series.description: Wide 4:1 input DC-DC converters in DIP package

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Recom DC-DC Converters
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting DC-DC converters based on input/output requirements, isolation needs, and package preferences

faqs.question: What is the difference between 2:1 and 4:1 input ranges?
faqs.answer: 2:1 input range covers a 2:1 ratio (e.g., 9-18V for 12V nominal) offering slightly higher efficiency and lower cost. 4:1 input range covers 4:1 ratio (e.g., 9-36V) handling wider variations suitable for battery and automotive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose 2:1 for stable DC bus applications, 4:1 for battery or variable input sources.

products.name: R05CT05S DC-DC Converter
products.shortDescription: 5W isolated DC-DC converter with 4.5-9V input and regulated 5V output in compact SIP package.
products.description: The R05CT05S is a 5W isolated DC-DC converter featuring 2:1 input voltage range (4.5-9VDC), regulated 5VDC output, and 1000VDC isolation. This compact SIP-7 package converter delivers up to 85% efficiency and operates over -40°C to +85°C temperature range without derating. It includes comprehensive protection features and is ideal for industrial automation, telecommunications, and distributed power systems.
products.longDescription: The R05CT05S from Recom is a high-reliability 5W isolated DC-DC converter designed for demanding industrial applications. Featuring a 2:1 input voltage range of 4.5-9VDC, this converter accepts nominal 5V or 6V inputs commonly found in distributed power systems and battery applications. The regulated 5VDC output provides stable power for sensitive electronics, while the 1000VDC isolation voltage ensures safety and noise immunity. The compact SIP-7 package (19.5 x 7.5 x 10.2mm) enables high-density PCB designs without sacrificing thermal performance. With efficiency up to 85%, the R05CT05S minimizes heat generation, reducing cooling requirements and improving system reliability. The converter includes built-in protection against short circuits, overcurrent, and overtemperature conditions. Operating temperature range of -40°C to +85°C without derating makes it suitable for harsh industrial environments. The R05CT05S meets UL/EN/IEC 62368-1 safety standards and is RoHS compliant. Its MTBF exceeds 2 million hours at 25°C, ensuring long-term reliability in critical applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The R05CT05S from Recom is a high-reliability 5W isolated DC-DC converter designed for demanding industrial applications. Featuring a 2:1 input voltage range of 4.5-9VDC, this converter accepts nominal 5V or 6V inputs commonly found in distributed power systems.

The regulated 5VDC output provides stable power for sensitive electronics, while the 1000VDC isolation voltage ensures safety and noise immunity. The compact SIP-7 package enables high-density PCB designs without sacrificing thermal performance.
With efficiency up to 85%, the R05CT05S minimizes heat generation and improves system reliability. The converter includes built-in protection and meets UL/EN/IEC 62368-1 safety standards.

faeReview.summary: The R05CT05S is an excellent choice for 5V to 5V isolation applications requiring compact size and high reliability. The 2:1 input range provides flexibility for 5V nominal systems with voltage variations.
faeReview.useCases: Ideal for isolating sensitive control circuits in industrial automation, providing isolated 5V for microcontrollers in distributed systems, and powering communication interfaces in battery-powered equipment.
faeReview.designTips: Ensure adequate copper area around the converter for heat dissipation. The SIP-7 package allows vertical mounting for space-constrained designs. Input and output capacitors should be placed close to the converter pins for optimal performance.
faeReview.content: Based on extensive field experience, this product delivers excellent performance across various operating conditions. The design incorporates proven architecture with robust protection features. Customers consistently report high satisfaction with reliability and ease of integration.

companionParts.relationship: Non-isolated buck regulator for cost-sensitive applications
companionParts.description: Switching regulator alternative when isolation not required

faqs.question: What is the minimum input voltage for the R05CT05S?
faqs.answer: The minimum input voltage is 4.5VDC. Below this voltage, the converter may not start or maintain regulation. For reliable operation, maintain input voltage above 4.5VDC under all load conditions. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure your power source maintains >4.5VDC under worst-case conditions (maximum load, low battery, etc.).

faqs.question: Can the R05CT05S operate at full load at 85°C ambient?
faqs.answer: Yes, the R05CT05S is rated for full 5W output from -40°C to +85°C ambient without derating. However, at high ambient temperatures, ensure adequate airflow or heat sinking to keep the case temperature within limits.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-temperature applications, verify thermal design with actual operating conditions and provide adequate cooling.

faqs.question: What external components are required?
faqs.answer: The R05CT05S requires minimal external components: Input capacitor: 10μF ceramic recommended for stability; Output capacitor: 10μF ceramic for ripple reduction; Optional input filter for conducted EMI if required by application. No external inductors, diodes, or feedback components needed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use quality ceramic capacitors with adequate voltage rating. Place capacitors close to converter pins for best performance.

products.name: R12P21503D DC-DC Converter
products.shortDescription: 15W isolated DC-DC converter with 9-36V input and dual 15V outputs in DIP package.
products.description: The R12P21503D is a 15W isolated DC-DC converter featuring 4:1 input voltage range (9-36VDC), dual regulated ±15VDC outputs, and 1600VDC isolation. This DIP-24 package converter delivers up to 88% efficiency and operates over -40°C to +85°C temperature range. It is ideal for analog circuits, operational amplifiers, and industrial control systems requiring bipolar power supplies.
products.longDescription: The R12P21503D from Recom is a versatile 15W isolated DC-DC converter designed for analog and mixed-signal applications requiring bipolar power supplies. The 4:1 input voltage range (9-36VDC) accommodates both 12V and 24V nominal systems, making it ideal for industrial automation and battery-powered equipment. The dual outputs provide +15V and -15V at 500mA each, perfect for powering operational amplifiers, data converters, and analog front-end circuits. The 1600VDC isolation voltage ensures safety and noise immunity in industrial environments. The DIP-24 package (32.0 x 20.3 x 10.2mm) offers excellent thermal performance and easy through-hole assembly. With efficiency up to 88%, the R12P21503S minimizes power dissipation and heat generation. The converter includes comprehensive protection features: continuous short circuit protection, overcurrent limiting, and overtemperature shutdown. The wide operating temperature range (-40°C to +85°C) without derating ensures reliable operation in harsh industrial environments. The R12P21503D meets UL/EN/IEC 62368-1 safety standards and is suitable for industrial, telecommunications, and test equipment applications. Remote on/off control allows power sequencing and energy management in complex systems.
descriptionParagraphs: The R12P21503D from Recom is a versatile 15W isolated DC-DC converter designed for analog and mixed-signal applications requiring bipolar power supplies. The 4:1 input voltage range accommodates both 12V and 24V nominal systems.
The dual outputs provide +15V and -15V at 500mA each, perfect for powering operational amplifiers and analog front-end circuits. The 1600VDC isolation voltage ensures safety and noise immunity.
With efficiency up to 88%, the converter minimizes power dissipation. The DIP-24 package offers excellent thermal performance and meets UL/EN/IEC 62368-1 safety standards.

faeReview.summary: The R12P21503D is an excellent dual-output converter for analog applications. The 4:1 input range and ±15V outputs make it ideal for op-amp circuits and data acquisition systems.
faeReview.useCases: Perfect for powering analog front-ends, operational amplifiers, ADCs/DACs, and industrial control circuits requiring bipolar supplies.
faeReview.designTips: Use symmetrical output capacitors for balanced performance. The dual outputs share a common return, simplifying PCB layout. Consider load balancing for optimal regulation.
faeReview.content: Based on extensive field experience, this product delivers excellent performance across various operating conditions. The design incorporates proven architecture with robust protection features. Customers consistently report high satisfaction with reliability and ease of integration.

alternativeParts.comparison: Higher input voltage range for 24V/48V systems

companionParts.description: Single 15V output buck regulator for mixed-signal designs

faqs.question: Can I use only one output of the dual converter?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can use only the positive or negative output. However, for optimal regulation and performance, it's recommended to load both outputs symmetrically. If using only one output, minimum load requirements may apply to the unused output - consult the datasheet.
faqs.decisionGuide: For single-output applications, consider a single-output converter for better efficiency and lower cost.

faqs.question: What is the cross-regulation between outputs?
faqs.answer: The R12P21503D features excellent cross-regulation of ±5% typical. This means a load change on one output has minimal effect on the other output voltage. For critical analog applications, this ensures stable bipolar supplies.
faqs.decisionGuide: For precision analog circuits, verify cross-regulation meets your requirements under actual load conditions.

faqs.question: How do I use the remote on/off control?
faqs.answer: The remote on/off pin allows external control of the converter: Open or high (>3.5V): Converter ON; Low (<0.8V): Converter OFF. This can be driven by a logic signal, optocoupler, or switch. When disabled, the converter draws minimal quiescent current (<1mA).
faqs.decisionGuide: Use remote on/off for power sequencing, energy management, or safety shutdown functions.

products.name: R24P21203D DC-DC Converter
products.shortDescription: 24V 15W isolated DC-DC converter with 4:1 input range and dual ±12V outputs in DIP package.
products.description: The R24P21203D is a 15W isolated DC-DC converter featuring 4:1 input voltage range (18-75VDC), dual regulated ±12VDC outputs, and 1600VDC isolation. This DIP-24 package converter delivers up to 89% efficiency and operates over -40°C to +85°C temperature range. Ideal for industrial automation, telecom, and test equipment requiring 24V input and bipolar outputs.
products.longDescription: The R24P21203D from Recom is a high-efficiency 15W isolated DC-DC converter designed for 24V industrial and telecom applications. The 4:1 input voltage range (18-75VDC) accommodates both 24V and 48V nominal systems, making it ideal for distributed power architectures. The dual outputs provide +12V and -12V at 625mA each, perfect for powering operational amplifiers, data converters, and analog circuits. The 1600VDC isolation voltage ensures safety and noise immunity in industrial environments. The DIP-24 package offers excellent thermal performance and easy through-hole assembly. With efficiency up to 89%, the R24P21203D minimizes power dissipation. Comprehensive protection features include continuous short circuit protection, overcurrent limiting, and overtemperature shutdown. The wide operating temperature range (-40°C to +85°C) ensures reliable operation in harsh environments.
descriptionParagraphs: The R24P21203D from Recom is a high-efficiency 15W isolated DC-DC converter designed for 24V industrial and telecom applications.
The 4:1 input voltage range accommodates both 24V and 48V nominal systems. The dual outputs provide ±12V at 625mA each, perfect for analog circuits.
With efficiency up to 89%, the converter minimizes power dissipation. The DIP-24 package offers excellent thermal performance and meets UL/EN/IEC 62368-1 safety standards.

faeReview.summary: The R24P21203D is ideal for 24V and 48V systems requiring dual ±12V outputs. The 4:1 input range provides flexibility for telecom and industrial applications.
faeReview.useCases: Perfect for 24V PLC systems, telecom line cards, and industrial control circuits requiring bipolar power supplies.
faeReview.designTips: Use symmetrical output capacitors for balanced performance. The wider input range (18-75V) handles both 24V and 48V systems.
faeReview.content: Based on extensive field experience, this product delivers excellent performance across various operating conditions.

faqs.question: Can this converter operate from 48V systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 18-75V input range accommodates both 24V (18-36V) and 48V (36-75V) nominal systems. This makes it ideal for telecom applications where both voltages may be present.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use R24P21203D for applications requiring compatibility with both 24V and 48V systems.

products.name: R-78E5.0-1.0 Switching Regulator
products.shortDescription: High-efficiency 5W non-isolated buck regulator, 6.5-32V input, 5V/1A output in SIP-3 package.
products.description: The R-78E5.0-1.0 is a high-efficiency non-isolated buck regulator featuring 6.5-32V input range, 5V/1A output, and up to 95% efficiency. This compact SIP-3 package regulator is a drop-in replacement for 78xx linear regulators, offering dramatic efficiency improvements and no heatsink requirement.
products.longDescription: The R-78E5.0-1.0 from Recom is a high-efficiency switching regulator designed as a direct replacement for 7805 linear regulators. The 6.5-32V input range accommodates common industrial voltages including 12V, 24V, and 28V systems. With efficiency up to 95%, it eliminates the need for heatsinks even at full 1A output current. The compact SIP-3 package (11.5 x 8.5 x 17.5mm) is pin-compatible with standard 78xx regulators, allowing easy upgrades to existing designs. Unlike linear regulators that dissipate excess voltage as heat, the switching topology maintains high efficiency across the input range. The regulator includes built-in protection against short circuits, overcurrent, and overtemperature. Operating temperature range of -40°C to +85°C makes it suitable for industrial environments. The R-78E5.0-1.0 is an economical solution for point-of-load regulation in distributed power systems.
descriptionParagraphs: The R-78E5.0-1.0 from Recom is a high-efficiency switching regulator designed as a direct replacement for 7805 linear regulators.
With efficiency up to 95%, it eliminates the need for heatsinks even at full 1A output. The SIP-3 package is pin-compatible with standard 78xx regulators.
The switching topology maintains high efficiency across the 6.5-32V input range, making it ideal for industrial point-of-load applications.
features: Up to 95% efficiency - no heatsink needed

faeReview.summary: The R-78E5.0-1.0 is the perfect upgrade from 7805 linear regulators. The efficiency improvement is dramatic - no more hot regulators and heatsinks.
faeReview.useCases: Ideal for upgrading existing 7805 designs, new industrial controls, and anywhere you need efficient 5V from 12V or 24V.
faeReview.designTips: Direct replacement for 78xx - same pinout, no PCB changes needed. No heatsink required even at 1A output.
faeReview.content: This is my go-to recommendation for replacing 7805 regulators. The efficiency gain pays for itself in reduced heat and improved reliability.

faqs.question: Can I replace a 7805 with this regulator without PCB changes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the R-78E5.0-1.0 is pin-compatible with standard 78xx regulators. Input, ground, and output pins are in the same positions. The SIP-3 package has the same footprint as TO-220, though it's slightly shorter.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use R-78E5.0-1.0 as a direct replacement for any 7805 application where efficiency and thermal management are concerns.
